title: Isaiah 24
subtitle: The Isaiah Apocalypse Begins: The Earth Devastated

sections.header: Verses 1–13 — “The Earth Is Defiled by Its People”

heb.gloss: to devastate / to empty / to lay waste
heb.paragraph: "See, the LORD is going to lay waste (bālaq) the earth and devastate it" (v.1). THE Isaiah Apocalypse (chs.24–27) — cosmic judgment beyond any single nation. The earth is emptied, scattered, stripped (v.1). Priest and people, master and servant, buyer and seller — all alike (v.2). The earth dries up; the world languishes (v.4). WHY? "The earth is defiled by its people; they have disobeyed the laws, violated the statutes and broken the everlasting covenant" (v.5). A CURSE consumes the earth (v.6). Joy gone: wine dries up, merry-making silenced, songs stopped, beer bitter (vv.7–11). The city of chaos is desolate (v.10). "Only a few are left, as when an olive tree is beaten" (v.13) — the remnant metaphor again.

notes.note: "The LORD is going to lay waste the earth. It will be the same for priest as for people, for master as for servant." No exceptions. No class immunity. Universal devastation.

"The earth is defiled by its people; they have broken the everlasting covenant. Therefore a curse consumes the earth." The "everlasting covenant" — probably the Noahic covenant (Gen 9). Humanity has violated the most basic creation-order. The curse of Genesis 3 reaches its culmination.
"The ruined city lies desolate. Gladness is banished from the earth. Only a few are left." The city of chaos (tohu — the same word as Genesis 1:2). Creation is un-created. But: a remnant remains.

sections.header: Verses 14–23 — “The LORD Will Reign on Mount Zion”

heb.gloss: he reigns / he has become king
heb.paragraph: "The LORD Almighty will reign (mālak̅) on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and before its elders — with great glory" (v.23). From the west the remnant raises their voices and shouts for joy (v.14). From the east: glory to the Righteous One (v.16). But Isaiah says: "Woe to me! The treacherous betray!" (v.16). Terror, pit, and snare await the inhabitants (v.17). The floodgates of the heavens open; the foundations of the earth shake (v.18) — the flood-imagery returns. The earth reels like a drunkard, sways like a hut in the wind (v.20). "In that day the LORD will punish the powers in the heavens above and the kings on the earth below" (v.21). Moon ashamed, sun disgraced — because the LORD’s glory outshines them (v.23).

notes.note: "From the ends of the earth we hear singing: ‘Glory to the Righteous One.’ But I said, ‘I waste away! Woe to me!’" The global praise is real AND the prophet’s anguish is real. Both coexist. The treachery hasn’t ended.

"The earth is broken up, the earth is split asunder, the earth is violently shaken. The earth reels like a drunkard." Three verbs of breaking; one simile of drunkenness. The planet itself is destabilised.

"The LORD will punish the powers in the heavens above and the kings on the earth below. The moon will be dismayed, the sun ashamed, when the LORD reigns on Mount Zion." Two-tier judgment: heavenly and earthly. Then God’s reign outshines sun and moon. Rev 21:23 develops this.

ppl.name: LORD
ppl.text: "The LORD is going to lay waste the earth…

ppl.name: servant
ppl.text: It will be the same for priest as for people, for master as for servant…

ppl.name: king
ppl.text: "The LORD will punish the powers in the heavens above and the kings on the earth below…

ppl.name: remnant
ppl.text: remnant is a key figure in The Isaiah Apocalypse Begins: The Earth Devastated.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVit will be the same for priest as for people, for the master as for his servant, for the mistress as for her servant, for seller as for buyer, for borrower as for lender, for debtor as for creditor.ESVAnd it shall be, as with the people, so with the priest; as with the slave, so with his master; as with the maid, so with her mistress; as with the buyer, so with the seller; as with the lender, so with the borrower; as with the creditor, so with the debtor.NIVTherefore a curse consumes the earth; its people must bear their guilt. Therefore earth’s inhabitants are burned up, and very few are left.ESVTherefore a curse devours the earth, and its inhabitants suffer for their guilt; therefore the inhabitants of the earth are scorched, and few men are left.

rec.text: ""The LORD is going to lay waste the earth. It will be the same for priest as for people, for master as for servant." No exceptions. No class immunity. Universal devastation."

rows.label: The LORD Reigns — 24:14–23
rows.text: Earth reels like a drunkard. "The LORD will REIGN on Mount Zion — with great GLORY." Moon ashamed; sun disgraced.

themes.note: Judgment peaks at 10: cosmic devastation, everlasting covenant broken, earth un-created. Sovereignty at 9: God reigns, outshining sun and moon. The Isaiah Apocalypse begins.
